title: Does any AI tool actually help you decide when NOT to trade — or just when to trade?
author: No_Cockroach_3231
selftext:
Every AI trading tool I’ve tried is built around the same thing — finding entries. Signals, screeners, pattern recognition, buy alerts.  
Nobody seems to be solving the other side. Whether the market environment actually deserves your capital right now.  
Regime wrong. Sentiment shifting. Narrative against your thesis. Setup looks perfect on the chart. You take it anyway because nothing is telling you to stay out.  
I’ve been trying to solve this manually every morning. Regime read, sentiment check, narrative assessment — before touching any chart. It takes time and I haven’t found anything that automates it properly.  
Has anyone found an AI tool that actually does this well? Or are we all still doing environment assessment manually?

comments.author: Charlie_Slaptail
comments.body: I am not sure about the entire market as I’m new to this, but there is a bot in beta now. Basically I ask it to read the tape and it gives important levels which would be what you’re wanting. It can do crypto but it will tell you up front it’s not accurate because of the manipulation.

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: Interesting — so it reads tape and gives key levels. Does it factor in the broader market regime at all, or is it purely price-based? That’s the part I haven’t seen any tool handle well — knowing whether the overall environment supports taking a position at all, not just where the levels are.

replies.author: Charlie_Slaptail
replies.body: Yes it factors in the broader market including news up to that point. Today it give caution due to the Middle East instability.

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: That’s really interesting — especially the geopolitical context. Does it give you a structured decision state like stay out entirely versus reduce size, or more of a general caution flag? That distinction matters a lot for actually acting on it.

replies.author: Charlie_Slaptail
replies.body: It doesn’t give a decision, it tells where the key levels will be and what could trigger them. I asked today what the capabilities will be for live alerts. Waiting for an answer.

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: That makes sense — levels and triggers are useful but they still leave the hardest part to you. Knowing whether the environment deserves a position at all before you even look at levels. That’s the gap I keep running into.

comments.author: MycoHost01
comments.body:
For perp trading there’s always a trade when it’s flat you either predict correct or you stay out. 

I have my own indexer for perp data on Solana there’s always an opportunity long or short in any condition there’s always a trade 

I have a macro and an event pipeline along with my indexer so it knows what’s happening with current events and looks at how it impacts the market is pretty cool next comes trading instructions since it got the analysis correct

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: That macro and event pipeline sounds genuinely impressive — especially combining it with current events impact analysis. Curious how you handle the regime side of it — do you have a systematic way to classify whether the overall market environment supports taking a position at all?

comments.author: rabbitee2
comments.body:
nobody's really automated regime detection well yet. closest i've seen is building your own composite from VIX term structure, breadth, and correlation clustering, then running it through a simple decision tree that flags no trade conditions. takes work upfront but it's honestly the only thing that's kept me from overtrading.

For watching what real traders actually sit out on ,the social feed on markets .xyz is intresting since every position is verified

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: That composite approach is exactly the right framework — VIX term structure plus breadth is a solid regime proxy. The decision tree piece is what most people skip. I’m actually building something that automates this entire layer. Early access is open if you want to try it — https://forms.gle/oJMXy6BfVMrBD8Jx6

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: That composite approach is exactly right — VIX term structure plus breadth is a solid regime proxy. The decision tree piece is what most people skip. I’m actually building something that automates this entire layer. DM me if you want early access.

comments.author: BuildwithPublic
comments.body:
This is exactly the problem I've been working on using r/PublicApp 

The entry side is a solved problem - screeners everywhere. Nobody's cracking pre-trade environment assessment at the individual trader level

What actually works with Claude + execution API: you build a morning regime agent that runs before you touch a chart. VIX term structure, put/call skew, SMA slope on your universe, whatever gates matter to your strategy. If the environment doesn't pass, the agent doesn't surface setups. Full stop.

The key is separating regime read from signal generation. Two different prompts, two different jobs. Most people collapse them and wonder why their "AI trading tool" keeps triggering in bad tape.

r/PublicApp has an MCP server that connects Claude directly to live options chains and execution - so the same agent that reads the environment can also manage the trade if conditions clear. Happy to help you get something set up. What does your current morning process actually look like?

replies.author: No_Cockroach_3231
replies.body: That separation is exactly right — regime read and signal generation are two completely different cognitive tasks and almost every tool collapses them. My morning process right now is manual — VIX structure, sector breadth, narrative scan before I touch any chart. Working on automating that layer specifically. What does your regime agent output — a binary go/no-go or something more graduated?

comments.author: StevenVinyl
comments.body:
yes. 

Cod3x does exactly this lol. AI reasons through live market data and your strategy and then decided whether to take a trade or not. You also get full reasoning logs of why, what when, etc... 

Can also automate the whole process or "vibetrade" as the lads call it today. 

  
Vid for ref: [https://x.com/StevenVinyl/status/2051722099580956903?s](https://x.com/StevenVinyl/status/2051722099580956903?s)

comments.author: OrangeSpectre
comments.body: I’ve been thinking the same thing tbh. most AI tools just give “signals” but don’t really help you decide when NOT to trade. I tested a few setups and even monkeytradepro, and it’s more like automation of your rules than real decision making. feels like edge still comes from your own strategy, not the AI itself.
